glossary.definition: The moment when a prototype or specification is passed from product/design to development. Critical decision point: fidelity (pixel-perfect build) or specification-only (developer interprets). Must be aligned before engineering starts.

A visual metric showing an entrepreneur's experience level. Displayed as 3 ascending buildings (Junior: 0-15 projects, Senior: 16-50 projects, Partner: 50+ projects). Helps contextualize advice from AI agents.

Defensible advantage — the unique structural barrier a startup builds that competitors cannot easily replicate. MOAT = Moat Of Architecture. Example: proprietary data, network effects, brand lock-in, switching costs.

Backend architecture decisions (Python vs Node.js, microservices vs monolith) do NOT determine user experience. UX is frontend-driven. Backend choice should serve speed, scalability, and team skill — not UX requirements.

Node.js: JavaScript runtime, async-first, great for I/O-heavy apps, rapid MVP. Python: mature ecosystem, ML/data science strength, cleaner syntax. Choice depends on team skill, project type, and scaling needs — not a binary choice.

Customer loyalty metric (0-100 scale). Score 9-10 = Promoter, 7-8 = Passive, 0-6 = Detractor. Formula: (Promoters - Detractors) / Total × 100. High NPS indicates product-market fit and customer satisfaction.
Call-to-action on first login = 'Meet your team'. Emotional, not transactional. Goal: establish psychological safety and team introduction before any task.
Call-to-action on returning users = 'Ask your team'. Operational, back-to-work framing. Goal: drive engagement with AI agents for daily decision-making.
When a conversation grows too long, the message history becomes a heavy load. System gets slow and wastes many tokens. Solution: start a new thread to reset context and maintain efficiency.

System waits a few seconds after you stop typing before saving. Does not save on every keystroke — prevents performance overload and saves compute costs. User feels natural flow; system stays efficient.

An independent, persistent conversation with continuous history. Threads don't mix — each topic gets its own thread. Easy to find past conversations, easy to hand off between AI agents without losing context.

A fixed time-boxed cycle (typically 1-2 weeks) where a team commits to a set of tasks and goals. At HypeShow: structured cycles where entrepreneurs work with AI agents toward defined outcomes. Tracked visually with progress bar and task count.

CSS property that clips content outside a container's boundaries. Used for SVG clipPath to hide portions of shapes. Prevents overflow and maintains clean layout when containing dynamic or oversized elements.

Brand colors: Coral (#e8623a, primary action), Dark (#1c1c1e, backgrounds), Gray (#6e6e73, secondary text), Purple (#7b3dd5, CPO/creative). Officer colors: Teal (Senior), Blue (Junior), Coral (Partner). Consistent across all interfaces and states (hover, active, error, empty).
Intelligent selection of which Claude model handles each task. Example: Haiku for lightweight decisions (7d), Sonnet for complex reasoning. Routes to save cost and latency. Improves efficiency ~60%.
System prompt caching with Anthropic API (2,500+ tokens). Saves 10% compute and speeds up context lookup. Cache hit flag = 90% cache efficiency. Reduces API latency and costs significantly.

AI manages conversation history by monitoring recent message tokens. When limit reached, sliding window drops oldest messages and keeps only relevant recent context. Prevents context bloat and memory overflow.

Soft throttling: gradual slowdown at thresholds (12→70%, 30→90%, 60→100%, 110%+→exit). User sees 'you're approaching limit' but can continue. Hard block: instant stop. Soft throttling is preferred for UX — gives users agency vs sudden cutoff.

Supabase = PostgreSQL + Auth + Row-Level Security (RLS) + Connection Pooling. Free tier supports ~20 concurrent users + real-time. Vercel Postgres = simpler but limited on security/scaling. Supabase chosen for RLS + multi-user concurrency.

Detailed spec of every UI element: colors, states, typography, spacing. No pixel-perfect 'quote' approach — engineers implement design language. Includes all states: default, hover, active, error, empty. Design system, not design files.

Specification that answers all engineering questions upfront: API endpoints, empty states, mobile behavior, routing logic, edge cases. Before Spec → handoff: Confirm: 'What do I build — fidelity or logic?' Clarity = velocity.
